retry_reason: put operation always executes on each present subnode; if a kobject_put fails (e.g., refcount already zero), retrying would cause a double-put and use-after-free risk.
contract_summary: This function recursively iterates over all non-device subnodes of an ACPI device data structure and releases each subnode's reference by calling kobject_put. It consumes the references that were presumably held by the parent. After return, the subnodes may be freed and must not be used. No reference is passed back to the caller; this is purely a release operation.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to decrement the reference count of all non-device subnodes via kobject_put, recursively. The only additional logic is a list_empty check and iteration; no other business logic.

check.reason: The put operation via kobject_put(&dn->kobj) acts on dn->kobj.kref.refcount. The access path dn.kobj.kref.refcount and type chain acpi_data_node--kobject--kref--refcount_t correctly trace from the local variable dn to the refcount field. Segment counts match (4 segments and 4 types). The recursive call is consistent with the callee info treating the function as a put on dn.
